rule.name: ESXi Lockdown Mode Disabled
rule.description: This detection identifies when Lockdown Mode is disabled on an ESXi host, which can indicate that a threat actor is attempting to weaken host security controls. Disabling Lockdown Mode allows broader remote access via SSH or the host client and may precede further malicious actions such as data exfiltration, lateral movement, or VM tampering.
rule.query: `esxi_syslog` Message IN ("*lockdownmode.disabled*", "*Administrator access to the host has been enabled*") | rex field=_raw "Z (?<dest>[\w\.]+)\s" | stats min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime count by dest Message | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | `esxi_lockdown_mode_disabled_filter`
false_positives: Limited false positives in most environments, however tune as needed.
